2604.08455.title: KnowU-Bench: Towards Interactive, Proactive, and Personalized Mobile Agent Evaluation
2604.08455.paper_summary: Personalized mobile agents that infer user preferences and calibrate proactive assistance hold great promise as everyday digital assistants, yet existing benchmarks fail to capture what this requires. Prior work evaluates preference recovery from static histories or intent prediction from fixed contexts. Neither tests whether an agent can elicit missing preferences through interaction, nor whether it can decide when to intervene, seek consent, or remain silent in a live GUI environment. We introduce KnowU-Bench, an online benchmark for personalized mobile agents built on a reproducible Android emulation environment, covering 42 general GUI tasks, 86 personalized tasks, and 64 proactive tasks. Unlike prior work that treats user preferences as static context, KnowU-Bench hides the user profile from the agent and exposes only behavioral logs, forcing genuine preference inference rather than context lookup. To support multi-turn preference elicitation, it instantiates an LLM-driven user simulator grounded in structured profiles, enabling realistic clarification dialogues and proactive consent handling. Beyond personalization, KnowU-Bench provides comprehensive evaluation of the complete proactive decision chain, including grounded GUI execution, consent negotiation, and post-rejection restraint, evaluated through a hybrid protocol combining rule-based verification with LLM-as-a-Judge scoring. Our experiments reveal a striking degradation: agents that excel at explicit task execution fall below 50% under vague instructions requiring user preference inference or intervention calibration, even for frontier models like Claude Sonnet 4.6. The core bottlenecks are not GUI navigation but preference acquisition and intervention calibration, exposing a fundamental gap between competent interface operation and trustworthy personal assistance.
2604.08455.paper_summary_zh: 個性化的移動代理能夠推斷用戶偏好並調整主動協助，作為日常數位助理展現出巨大的潛力，然而現有的基準無法捕捉這所需的條件。先前的研究評估了從靜態歷史中恢復偏好或從固定上下文中預測意圖的能力。這兩者都未測試代理是否能通過互動引出缺失的偏好，也未測試其是否能決定何時介入、尋求同意或在實時GUI環境中保持沉默。我們介紹了KnowU-Bench，這是一個針對個性化移動代理的在線基準，建立在可重現的Android模擬環境上，涵蓋42個一般GUI任務、86個個性化任務和64個主動任務。與先前將用戶偏好視為靜態上下文的研究不同，KnowU-Bench將用戶檔案隱藏於代理之外，僅暴露行為日誌，迫使進行真實的偏好推斷而非上下文查找。為了支持多輪偏好引導，它實現了一個基於結構化檔案的LLM驅動用戶模擬器，能夠進行現實的澄清對話和主動同意處理。除了個性化，KnowU-Bench還提供了完整主動決策鏈的綜合評估，包括基於實際的GUI執行、同意協商和拒絕後的克制，通過結合基於規則的驗證與LLM作為評分者的混合協議進行評估。我們的實驗顯示出驚人的退化：在需要用戶偏好推斷或介入調整的模糊指令下，表現優異的代理在明確任務執行中卻未能達到50%的水平，即使對於像Claude Sonnet 4.6這樣的前沿模型也是如此。核心瓶頸不在於GUI導航，而在於偏好獲取和介入調整，揭示了能夠熟練操作界面與值得信賴的個人協助之間的根本差距。
2604.08455.author: Tongbo Chen et.al.
2604.08455.authors: Tongbo Chen, Zhengxi Lu, Zhan Xu, Guocheng Shao, Shaohan Zhao, Fei Tang, Yong Du, Kaitao Song, Yizhou Liu, Yuchen Yan, Wenqi Zhang, Xu Tan, Weiming Lu, Jun Xiao, Yueting Zhuang, Yongliang Shen
